contentBlocks.original:
भजे विशेषसुन्दरं समस्तपापखण्डनम् ।
स्वभक्तचित्तरञ्जनं सदैव राममद्वयम् ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhajē viśēṣasundaraṃ samastapāpakhaṇḍanam ।
svabhaktachittarañjanaṃ sadaiva rāmamadvayam ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I worship the uniquely beautiful Rama, destroyer of all sins, who eternally delights the minds of his devotees — the non-dual Rama.

contentBlocks.original:
जटाकलापशोभितं समस्तपापनाशकम् ।
स्वभक्तभीतिभञ्जनं भजे ह राममद्वयम् ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jaṭākalāpaśōbhitaṃ samastapāpanāśakam ।
svabhaktabhītibhañjanaṃ bhajē ha rāmamadvayam ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Adorned with a glorious crown of matted locks, destroyer of all sins, who shatters the fears of his devotees — I worship the non-dual Rama.

contentBlocks.original:
निजस्वरूपबोधकं कृपाकरं भवाऽपहम् ।
समं शिवं निरञ्जनं भजे ह राममद्वयम् ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nijasvarūpabōdhakaṃ kṛpākaraṃ bhavā'paham ।
samaṃ śivaṃ nirañjanaṃ bhajē ha rāmamadvayam ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Who reveals the nature of one's own true Self, the ever-gracious one, remover of the cycle of worldly existence — the equal, the auspicious, the untainted — I worship the non-dual Rama.

contentBlocks.original:
सदा प्रपञ्चकल्पितं ह्यनामरूपवास्तवम् ।
निराकृतिं निरामयं भजे ह राममद्वयम् ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sadā prapañchakalpitaṃ hyanāmarūpavāstavam ।
nirākṛtiṃ nirāmayaṃ bhajē ha rāmamadvayam ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: That which is always imagined as the universe, yet in reality has no name or form — formless, without affliction — I worship the non-dual Rama.

contentBlocks.original:
निष्प्रपञ्च निर्विकल्प निर्मलं निरामयम् ।
चिदेकरूपसन्ततं भजे ह राममद्वयम् ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
niṣprapañcha nirvikalpa nirmalaṃ nirāmayam ।
chidēkarūpasantataṃ bhajē ha rāmamadvayam ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Free from entanglement in the world, free from thought-constructs (nirvikalpa), spotless, without affliction — eternally of the nature of pure consciousness alone — I worship the non-dual Rama.

contentBlocks.original:
भवाब्धिपोतरूपकं ह्यशेषदेहकल्पितम् ।
गुणाकरं कृपाकरं भजे ह राममद्वयम् ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhavābdhipōtarūpakaṃ hyaśēṣadēhakalpitam ।
guṇākaraṃ kṛpākaraṃ bhajē ha rāmamadvayam ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The ship to cross the ocean of becoming, whose form encompasses all beings — treasure-house of all virtues, the ever-gracious one — I worship the non-dual Rama.

contentBlocks.original:
महासुवाक्यबोधकैर्विराजमानवाक्पदैः ।
परं च ब्रह्म व्यापकं भजे ह राममद्वयम् ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahāsuvākyabōdhakairvirājamānavākpadaiḥ ।
paraṃ cha brahma vyāpakaṃ bhajē ha rāmamadvayam ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Made luminously known by the great Mahavakyas (Vedic proclamations of truth), by radiant and noble words — the supreme Brahman, all-pervading — I worship the non-dual Rama.

contentBlocks.original:
शिवप्रदं सुखप्रदं भवच्छिदं भ्रमापहम् ।
विराजमानदैशिकं भजे ह राममद्वयम् ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śivapradaṃ sukhapradaṃ bhavachChidaṃ bhramāpaham ।
virājamānadaiśikaṃ bhajē ha rāmamadvayam ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Bestower of auspiciousness and happiness, cutter of bondage, dispeller of delusion — who shines glorious as the Guru-teacher of all — I worship the non-dual Rama.

contentBlocks.original:
रामाष्टकं पठति यः सुखदं सुपुण्यं
व्यासेन भाषितमिदं शृणुते मनुष्यः ।
विद्यां श्रियं विपुलसौख्यमनन्तकीर्तिं
सम्प्राप्य देहविलये लभते च मोक्षम् ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāmāṣṭakaṃ paṭhati yaḥ sukhadaṃ supuṇyaṃ
vyāsēna bhāṣitamidaṃ śṛṇutē manuṣyaḥ ।
vidyāṃ śriyaṃ vipulasaukhyamanantakīrtiṃ
samprāpya dēhavilayē labhatē cha mōkṣam ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever recites or hears this Ramashtakam — auspicious, rich in merit, spoken by Vyasa — attains knowledge, prosperity, boundless happiness, and eternal liberation. (Phalashruti)
Thus ends the Sri Ramashtakam proclaimed by Sri Vyasa. (Colophon)
